description_long: This is a tall, slightly irregular white candle with a visible flame at the top, indicating it is currently lit. The candle's body is uneven, with subtle bulges and indentations, giving it a hand-molded or partially melted appearance. The base is wider and rounded, providing stability, while the upper section tapers gently toward the flame. The wick is clearly visible at the center of the flame, and there are faint traces of melted wax running down the sides. The surface has a matte, semi-soft look typical of paraffin or beeswax candles. No external holder or base is present, suggesting the candle is designed to stand on its own.
description: A tall, white, slightly irregular candle with a visible flame and wick, featuring a rounded base and subtle wax drips.
description_view_0: Front view shows the candle's rounded base, visible flame, and a slightly indented body with minor wax drips.
description_view_1: This side view highlights the uneven, organic shape of the candle and the flame at the top, with more pronounced surface irregularities.
description_view_2: The back view displays the tapering body and rounded base, with the wick and flame clearly visible at the top.
description_view_3: Another side view, similar to view 1, showing the candle's irregular surface and the flame, with less visible wax drips.
